---
title: "Instructor Notes"
---

Cross-episode guidance for everyone teaching ForgeLab lessons. Per-episode pitfalls live in
each episode's "Instructor note" callouts.

## Before the workshop

- Confirm **co-teaching**: at least 2 instructors + 1–2 helpers. Never teach alone.
- Assign roles, especially online: **lead facilitator**, **chat/Q&A monitor**, **tech/breakout support**.
- Test the cloud fallback (Codespaces) yourself — it's your safety net when local setup fails.
- Open the collaborative notes doc (HackMD/Etherpad) and pin the link.
- Send the [pre-survey](https://github.com/frankvl/ForgeLab-Carpentries/blob/main/surveys/pre-workshop.md) ahead of time.

## During — the core loop

Run tight cycles: **short demo (live code) → learner challenge → debrief**. Online, make demos
even shorter and exercises more frequent to combat fatigue.

- **Type, don't paste.** Learners follow your fingers. Narrate what and why.
- **Make mistakes on purpose** occasionally and recover — it models debugging.
- **Check for understanding** with formative feedback (sticky notes in person; emoji/poll online).
- **Helpers circulate** (in person) or watch chat/breakouts (online) and surface blockers.
- **Equity in call-outs** — use random or round-robin selection, never the same hands.

## Formative feedback signals

- **Red/green sticky notes** (in person): green = done, red = stuck. Don't move on while reds are up.
- **Minute cards / one-up-one-down** at breaks: one thing that worked, one to improve.
- Adjust pace live based on signals — the schedule serves the learners, not vice versa.

## Accessibility

- Enable captions online (auto + a human watching for errors).
- Increase font size; high-contrast theme; read aloud what you type.
- Share the reference/cheat sheet link early and often.

## After the workshop

- Send the [post-survey](https://github.com/frankvl/ForgeLab-Carpentries/blob/main/surveys/post-workshop.md) immediately.
- Schedule the [3–6 month follow-up](https://github.com/frankvl/ForgeLab-Carpentries/blob/main/surveys/followup-3-6-month.md).
- Run a [retrospective](https://github.com/frankvl/ForgeLab-Carpentries/blob/main/community/retrospective-template.md) with the teaching team.
- File issues/PRs for anything in the lessons that tripped learners up. This is how we improve.
